title: LiveMX supported more than 70 projects on music export, live venues, and digital circulation
content:
Between 2023 and 2026 LiveMX fostered a more diverse and sustainable music sector with a focus on three key areas:

  • music export
  • live music venues
  • digital circulation and engagement

    • Supported by Creative Europe under its Music Moves Europe initiative, LiveMX has redistributed through “cascading grants” €4 million to 72 projects selected through open calls for proposals.

    The selected projects brought together organisations from 26 countries, with activities resonating across more than 40 countries. The calls attracted more than 1 000 applications, showing the high interest and need for this kind of initiatives in the music sector.

    Projects reported tangible benefits, including

  • strengthened cross-border collaboration and network-building
  • greater inclusion and diversity
  • giving more space to marginalised artists/professionals

    • Sustainable practices were also tested, such as slow-touring or testing solutions for energy-efficient venues.

    The project implemented a capacity building programme and an innovative funding scheme to address specific needs within the European music ecosystem.

    Link to LiveMX's website.
snippet:
Between 2023 and 2026 LiveMX fostered a more diverse and sustainable music sector with a focus on three key areas:

music export

live music venues

digital circulation and engagement
creator: csudaga

title: Here is the third edition of Perform Europe
content:
After the publication of the call for proposals, a new consortium has been selected to implement the next phase of Perform Europe, the EU’s scheme that supports touring models in performing arts.

Building on the achievements of the first two phases of Perform Europe, this new phase will identify, and support touring and distribution of at least 25 artistic projects funded through ‘cascading grants’ over the next 24 months. Perform Europe III will receive a grant of €2 million from the Creative Europe programme - Cultural strand.

This funding will enable the projects to engage their audiences through more inclusive and sustainable dissemination methods, benefitting from financial assistance, training and peer-to-peer learning. A digital platform will centralise and promote the supported projects, while also serving as a networking and matchmaking tool.

The objectives of this phase of the scheme are to reduce environmental impact, strengthen fair working conditions, and enhance access for a diversity of audiences.

Perform Europe III will be carried out by a consortium of partners:

  • IETM – the International network for contemporary performing arts
  • European Festivals Association (EFA)
snippet: After the publication of the call for proposals, a new consortium has been selected to implement the next phase of Perform Europe, the EU’s scheme that supports touring models in performing arts.
creator: csudaga

title: Culture Moves Europe: 150 residency projects selected
content:
The combined budget of the call is more than €2.1 million. The grants per project depend on the duration (between 21 and 90 days) and the number of invited artists and cultural professionals (from 1 to 5).

The selected residency projects plan to host 581 artists and cultural professionals from different Creative Europe countries, which are selected in the second phase of the selection procedure. The submissions are reviewed on a rolling basis.

Selected hosts have until 30 September 2026 to select and confirm their residents. In the meantime, projects with early implementation dates can already start from early June 2026. All projects must be concluded by 7 June 2027.

Goethe-Institut is organising online match-making sessions on 5 and 10 June to bring together residency hosts and potential residents.

The 2025-2026 call for residency hosts received 1 326 applications from 38 Creative Europe countries. There will be match-making sessions in early June to bring together residency hosts and potential residents.

The selected residencies are based in 38 countries, including Moldova, that joined the Creative Europe programme in January 2026.

title: World Environment Day: reconnecting with nature through culture
content:
A mural about the ocean, experiencing forest through a creative lens, growing nature in former industrial spaces: across Europe, artists are finding new ways to reconnect people with the environment. 

The European Union supports these initiatives, helping transform ideas into projects with a lasting impact on people, places and nature. On World Environment Day, we put the spotlight on three Creative Europe-funded projects whose work is making a difference on the ground.

Working across coastal regions in Italy, Greece and Portugal, BLUETOUR brought together artists, cultural organisations and local communities to explore how tourism can help protect local heritage and ecosystems rather than put pressure on them.

The project resulted in a series of artworks rooted in local landscapes and identities. In Faro, the mural Cycles by Los Pepes Studio celebrates the rhythms of the Atlantic Ocean while, in Aegean Sea, artist Paolo Lolicata created Poetic Tour of Lesvos, an artistic map encouraging visitors to discover the island through its landscapes and literary heritage.

Together, these works invite residents and visitors alike to engage with places more thoughtfully and sustainably.
snippet: A mural about the ocean, experiencing forest through a creative lens, growing nature in former industrial spaces: across Europe, artists are finding new ways to reconnect people with the environment.
creator: csudaga
